---
title: "PracticalBayes vs Turing.jl — Benchmark Report"
subtitle: "Auto-generated from bench/results/*.jsonl — do not edit by hand"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
---

Latest PracticalBayes run: **2026-07-07T13:21:58.403**, commit `5bda4dd2`.
  
Latest Turing reference: **2026-07-07T11:47:22.671**, commit `5846bced`.

Timings report **first-call** (includes JIT compilation) separately from **steady-state median** — see `bench/suite.jl` for methodology (hand-rolled fixed-repetition timing, not BenchmarkTools/Chairmarks, so expensive configurations still get a real sample size instead of degenerating to N=1).

## Latest snapshot

### Layer: `logdensity`

| Model | Shape | Precision | Backend | PB first-call | PB median | Turing first-call | Turing median | PB/Turing (median) |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| normal | large | Float32 | none | 784.9 µs | 1.088 ms | 766.8 µs | 726.5 µs | 1.5× |

| normal | tiny | Float32 | none | 3.1 µs | 0.65 µs | 3.8 µs | 0.4 µs | 1.62× |
| normal | tiny | Float64 | none | 2.8 µs | 0.6 µs | 4.8 µs | 0.5 µs | 1.2× |
| poisson | k5_n2000 | Float64 | none | 357.5 µs | 189.05 µs | 155.7 µs | 159.95 µs | 1.18× |
| poisson | k5_n50000 | Float64 | none | 5.526 ms | 5.398 ms | 4.123 ms | 4.19 ms | 1.29× |

## Model corpus benchmarks

Latest PracticalBayes corpus run: **2026-07-07T22:13:34.606**, commit `00827ea2`, 55 distinct models.
  
Latest Turing corpus run (representative subset): **2026-07-07T22:38:01.260**, commit `00827ea2`, 20 distinct models.

Every model in `bench/corpus/posteriordb/` and `bench/corpus/tutorials/` (see `bench/corpus_bench.jl`), across every AD backend available in the benchmarking environment, Float64 and Float32, on three layers (logdensity, gradient, a short NUTS run). Turing only covers a representative subset (~20 models spanning each structural family — porting all ~53 to Turing as well was judged not worth the effort vs. the coverage gained). Enzyme is excluded from this sweep: across this many structurally varied models it produced an uncatchable crash that killed a full benchmark run outright (see `bench/corpus_bench.jl`'s comment) — it remains in `bench/suite.jl`'s smaller hand-picked sweep, where it's known to behave.

### Corpus layer: `logdensity`

### Corpus layer: `nuts`

| tutorials | probabilistic-pca | Float32 | ForwardDiff | 99.731 ms | — | — |
| tutorials | probabilistic-pca | Float64 | ForwardDiff | 115.811 ms | 333.236 ms | 0.35× |

## Reproducing

```
julia --project=. bench/suite.jl                                    # PracticalBayes side, appends to bench/results/history.jsonl
julia --project=test/comparison_env test/comparison_env/generate_turing_reference.jl  # Turing side, appends to bench/results/history_turing.jsonl
julia --project=bench/bench_env bench/corpus_bench.jl               # PracticalBayes corpus, appends to bench/results/history_corpus.jsonl
julia --project=test/comparison_env test/comparison_env/corpus_bench_turing.jl  # Turing corpus subset, appends to bench/results/history_corpus_turing.jsonl
julia --project=. bench/generate_report.jl                          # regenerate this file
quarto render bench/report.qmd                                      # render to HTML
```
